---
title: "DEV_R038: Derivation Core Function Pattern"
subtitle: "Extract shared derivation logic into core functions with thin platform wrappers"
chapter: "CH03"
category: "rule"
number: "DEV_R038"
date-created: "2025-12-27"
date-modified: "2025-12-27"
author: "Claude"
type: "rule"
law: "Development Methodology Law"
article-number:
  number: 38
  title: "Derivation Core Function Pattern"
related_to:
  - "DEV_R037: Derivation Step Isolation"
  - "DM_R044: Derivation Implementation Standard"
  - "DEV_R011: Derivation Platform Independence"
  - "SO_R007: One Function One File"
  - "MP064: ETL-Derivation Separation"
  - "MP145: Documentation-Implementation Alignment"
format:
  html:
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 3
    code-fold: false
    code-tools: true
    number-sections: true
---

# Article 38: Derivation Core Function Pattern {#overview}

**Development Methodology Law Article 38 (Derivation Core Function Pattern)**

This rule establishes the "core function + platform wrapper" architecture for derivation scripts that share logic across multiple platforms, ensuring code reuse while maintaining platform-specific configuration.

:::{.callout-important}
## Core Rule

1. When derivation logic is needed in **2+ platforms**, extract to a **core function**
2. Core functions go in `global_scripts/16_derivations/fn_D{group}_{seq}_core.R`
3. Platform wrappers are **thin** (~80 lines): init, source core, call with platform_id, deinit
4. **NO business logic in wrappers** - all logic in core function
:::

# Section 1: When to Apply {#when-to-apply}

## Subsection 1: Extraction Criteria {#extraction-criteria}

Extract to a core function when:

| Criterion | Threshold | Example |
|-----------|-----------|---------|
| **Platform count** | 2+ platforms need same logic | cbz, amz, eby all need DNA analysis |
| **Logic complexity** | > 50 lines of business logic | analysis_dna() calls, transformations |
| **Algorithm involvement** | Uses specific algorithms | DNA analysis, RFM calculation |
| **Maintenance concern** | Changes would need multi-file updates | Schema changes, business rules |

## Subsection 2: When NOT to Extract {#when-not-to-extract}

Keep platform-specific scripts when:

- Logic is **fundamentally different** per platform
- Platform has **unique data schema** that cannot be normalized
- Script is **simple** (< 30 lines, no complex logic)
- **One-off** implementation for a single platform

# Section 2: Architecture Pattern {#architecture}

## Subsection 1: Directory Structure {#directory-structure}

```
global_scripts/
├── 16_derivations/                   # Cross-company core functions (shared via subrepo)
│   ├── fn_D01_03_core.R             # Core function for D01_03 (DNA Analysis)
│   ├── fn_D01_04_core.R             # Core function for D01_04 (Customer Profile)
│   ├── fn_D01_05_core.R             # Core function for D01_05 (App Views)
│   └── README.md                    # Directory documentation

update_scripts/DRV/
├── all/                              # All-platform orchestration scripts
│   └── all_D01_06.R                 # Master orchestration (NOT a core function)
├── cbz/                              # Cyberbiz platform
│   ├── cbz_D01_03.R                 # Thin wrapper -> calls 16_derivations/fn_D01_03_core.R
│   ├── cbz_D01_04.R                 # Thin wrapper -> calls 16_derivations/fn_D01_04_core.R
│   └── cbz_D01_05.R                 # Thin wrapper -> calls 16_derivations/fn_D01_05_core.R
├── amz/                              # Amazon platform
│   ├── amz_D01_03.R                 # Thin wrapper -> calls 16_derivations/fn_D01_03_core.R
│   ├── amz_D01_04.R                 # Thin wrapper
│   └── amz_D01_05.R                 # Thin wrapper
└── eby/                              # eBay platform
    ├── eby_D01_03.R                 # Thin wrapper -> calls 16_derivations/fn_D01_03_core.R
    ├── eby_D01_04.R                 # Thin wrapper
    └── eby_D01_05.R                 # Thin wrapper
```

## Subsection 2: Core Function Location {#core-function-location}

| Logic Type | Location | Naming | Shared Via |
|------------|----------|--------|------------|
| **Cross-company derivation core** | `global_scripts/16_derivations/fn_D{group}_{seq}_core.R` | `fn_D01_03_core.R` | Git subrepo |
| **General utilities** | `global_scripts/04_utils/fn_{name}.R` | `fn_analysis_dna.R` | Git subrepo |
| **Transform functions** | `global_scripts/17_transform/fn_{name}.R` | `fn_transform_sales.R` | Git subrepo |

**Why `global_scripts/16_derivations/`?**

1. **Cross-company sharing**: Core functions are parameterized and work for ALL companies (MAMBA, BrandEdge, etc.)
2. **Subrepo sync**: `global_scripts/` is shared via git subrepo, so all companies automatically get updates
3. **Single source of truth**: One core function serves all platforms across all companies
4. **Clean separation**: Cores (business logic) in `16_derivations/`, algorithms in `04_utils/`

# Section 3: Implementation Templates {#templates}

## Subsection 1: Core Function Template {#core-function-template}

```r
#' @title D{group}_{seq} Core Function - {Description} (Cross-Company)
#' @description Core logic for {description}, used by platform-specific wrappers.
#'              Follows DEV_R038: Core Function + Platform Wrapper Pattern.
#'              Parameterized for cross-company use via git subrepo.
#' @param platform_id Character. Platform identifier (e.g., "cbz", "amz", "eby")
#' @param config Optional list. Platform config from app_config.yaml. If NULL, uses defaults.
#' @return List with success status and summary
#' @principle DEV_R038, DEV_R037, DM_R044

run_D{group}_{seq} <- function(platform_id, config = NULL) {

  # Validate platform_id is provided
  if (missing(platform_id) || is.null(platform_id) || !nzchar(platform_id)) {
    stop("platform_id is required")
  }

  # ===========================================================================
  # Configuration: Use config parameter or defaults
  # ===========================================================================

  # Default table patterns (can be overridden by config)
  input_table_pattern <- if (!is.null(config$input_table_pattern)) {
    config$input_table_pattern
  } else {
    "df_%s_sales___standardized"  # %s will be replaced with platform_id
  }

  output_table <- if (!is.null(config$output_table)) {
    config$output_table
  } else {
    "df_dna_by_customer___cleansed"  # Cross-platform table (no platform prefix)
  }

  # Build actual table name from pattern
  input_table <- sprintf(input_table_pattern, platform_id)

  # ===========================================================================
  # Initialize tracking
  # ===========================================================================

  error_occurred <- FALSE
  start_time <- Sys.time()
  rows_processed <- 0

  # ===========================================================================
  # MAIN: Business logic here
  # ===========================================================================

  tryCatch({
    message(sprintf("[%s] Processing from %s...", platform_id, input_table))

    # All business logic goes here
    # ... business logic ...

    rows_processed <- nrow(output_data)

  }, error = function(e) {
    error_occurred <<- TRUE
    message(sprintf("[%s] ERROR: %s", platform_id, e$message))
  })

  # ===========================================================================
  # Return summary
  # ===========================================================================

  execution_time <- as.numeric(difftime(Sys.time(), start_time, units = "secs"))

  return(list(
    success = !error_occurred,
    platform_id = platform_id,
    rows_processed = rows_processed,
    execution_time_secs = execution_time
  ))
}
```

## Subsection 2: Platform Wrapper Template {#wrapper-template}

```r
#!/usr/bin/env Rscript
#####
# DERIVATION: D{group}_{seq} {Description} ({PLATFORM})
# VERSION: 3.0
# PLATFORM: {platform}
# GROUP: D{group}
# SEQUENCE: {seq}
# PURPOSE: Execute {description} via core function
# CORE_FUNCTION: global_scripts/16_derivations/fn_D{group}_{seq}_core.R
# CONSUMES: {input_tables}
# PRODUCES: {output_tables}
# DEPENDS_ON_ETL: {platform}_ETL_{datatype}_{phase}
# DEPENDS_ON_DRV: {dependencies}
# PRINCIPLE: MP064, DEV_R037, DEV_R038, DM_R044
#####
#{platform}_D{group}_{seq}

# ==============================================================================
# PART 1: INITIALIZE
# ==============================================================================

if (!exists("autoinit", mode = "function")) {
  source(file.path("scripts", "global_scripts", "22_initializations", "sc_Rprofile.R"))
}

autoinit()

error_occurred <- FALSE
test_passed <- FALSE
start_time <- Sys.time()
platform_id <- "{platform}"

core_path <- file.path(GLOBAL_DIR, "16_derivations", "fn_D{group}_{seq}_core.R")
if (!file.exists(core_path)) {
  stop("Missing CORE_FUNCTION: global_scripts/16_derivations/fn_D{group}_{seq}_core.R")
}
source(core_path)

# ==============================================================================
# PART 2: MAIN
# ==============================================================================

result <- NULL
tryCatch({
  result <- run_D{group}_{seq}(platform_id = platform_id)
  test_passed <- isTRUE(result$success)
}, error = function(e) {
  error_occurred <<- TRUE
  message(sprintf("MAIN: ERROR - %s", e$message))
})

# ==============================================================================
# PART 3: TEST
# ==============================================================================

if (!error_occurred && !test_passed) {
  message("TEST: Core function reported failure")
}

# ==============================================================================
# PART 4: SUMMARIZE
# ==============================================================================

execution_time <- as.numeric(difftime(Sys.time(), start_time, units = "secs"))
message("SUMMARY: ", ifelse(!error_occurred && test_passed, "SUCCESS", "FAILED"))
message(sprintf("SUMMARY: Platform: %s", platform_id))
message(sprintf("SUMMARY: Execution time (secs): %.2f", execution_time))

# ==============================================================================
# PART 5: DEINITIALIZE
# ==============================================================================

autodeinit()
# NO STATEMENTS AFTER THIS LINE
```

## Subsection 3: Header Requirements {#header-requirements}

Platform wrappers MUST include:

| Field | Required | Description |
|-------|----------|-------------|
| `DERIVATION` | Yes | Step ID and description |
| `PLATFORM` | Yes | Platform identifier (cbz, amz, eby) |
| `CONSUMES` | Yes | Input table(s) |
| `PRODUCES` | Yes | Output table(s) |
| `DEPENDS_ON_ETL` | If applicable | ETL dependencies |
| `DEPENDS_ON_DRV` | If applicable | DRV dependencies |
| `CORE_FUNCTION` | **Yes** | Core function filename |
| `PRINCIPLE` | Yes | Must include DEV_R038 |

# Section 4: Core Function Design {#core-function-design}

## Subsection 1: Parameter Design {#parameter-design}

The `platform_id` parameter enables:

```r
# Platform-specific configuration lookup
config <- platform_config[[platform_id]]

# Platform-specific input/output tables
input_table <- sprintf("df_%s_sales___transformed", platform_id)
output_table <- sprintf("df_%s_customer_dna___cleansed", platform_id)

# Platform-specific logging
message(sprintf("[%s] Processing %d rows...", platform_id, nrow(data)))
```

## Subsection 2: Configuration Object {#configuration-object}

Structure the platform config for extensibility:

```r
platform_config <- list(
  cbz = list(
    # Database settings
    input_db = "transformed_data",
    output_db = "cleansed_data",

    # Table patterns (in order of preference)
    input_tables = c("df_cbz_sales___transformed", "df_cbz_sales_transformed"),

    # Field mappings (normalize to standard names)
    time_field = "order_created_at",
    amount_field = "total_price_after_discounts",
    customer_id_field = "customer_id",

    # Platform-specific options
    skip_columns = c("internal_id"),
    custom_filters = list(status = "completed")
  ),
  # ... more platforms
)
```

## Subsection 3: Field Normalization {#field-normalization}

Core functions should normalize platform-specific fields:

```r
normalize_fields <- function(data, config, platform_id) {
  # Map platform-specific fields to standard names
  result <- data

  # Time field -> payment_time
  if (config$time_field %in% names(result)) {
    result$payment_time <- result[[config$time_field]]
  }

  # Amount field -> total_spent
  if (config$amount_field %in% names(result)) {
    result$total_spent <- as.numeric(result[[config$amount_field]])
  }

  # Add platform identifier
  result$platform_id <- platform_id

  return(result)
}
```

# Section 5: Wrapper Constraints {#wrapper-constraints}

## Subsection 1: What Wrappers CAN Do {#wrapper-allowed}

```r
# Allowed in wrappers:
autoinit()                                    # Initialize
source(core_fn_path)                          # Source core function
result <- run_D01_03(platform_id = "cbz")     # Call core function
.InitEnv$result_summary <- result             # Store result
autodeinit()                                  # Cleanup
```

## Subsection 2: What Wrappers CANNOT Do {#wrapper-prohibited}

```r
# PROHIBITED in wrappers:

# NO data manipulation
df <- tbl2(con, "table") %>% collect()        # Move to core function
df <- df %>% filter(status == "active")       # Move to core function

# NO business logic
score <- calculate_rfm(df)                    # Move to core function
df$segment <- assign_segment(df$score)        # Move to core function

# NO conditional logic based on data
if (nrow(df) > 1000) { ... }                  # Move to core function

# NO loops or iterations
for (i in 1:n) { ... }                        # Move to core function
lapply(items, process)                        # Move to core function
```

# Section 6: Benefits {#benefits}

## Subsection 1: Single Source of Truth {#single-source}

```
Before DEV_R038:
cbz_D01_03.R: 150 lines of DNA logic
amz_D01_03.R: 145 lines of DNA logic (slightly different)
eby_D01_03.R: 148 lines of DNA logic (slightly different)
PROBLEM: 3 files to maintain, bugs fixed in one may not be fixed in others

After DEV_R038:
fn_D01_03_core.R: 300 lines of DNA logic (authoritative)
cbz_D01_03.R: 60 lines (wrapper only)
amz_D01_03.R: 60 lines (wrapper only)
eby_D01_03.R: 60 lines (wrapper only)
BENEFIT: Single file contains all logic, wrappers are trivial
```

## Subsection 2: Easier Testing {#easier-testing}

```r
# Test core function independently
test_that("D01_03 core function handles all platforms", {
  for (platform in c("cbz", "amz", "eby")) {
    result <- run_D01_03(platform_id = platform)
    expect_true(result$success)
    expect_true(result$rows_processed > 0)
  }
})
```

## Subsection 3: Clear Separation of Concerns {#separation}

| Concern | Handled By |
|---------|-----------|
| Business logic | Core function |
| Platform configuration | Core function's platform_config |
| Initialization/cleanup | Wrapper |
| Pipeline integration | Wrapper headers |
| Execution entry point | Wrapper |

# Section 7: Relationship to Other Principles {#relationships}

| Principle | Relationship |
|-----------|-------------|
| **DEV_R037** | Step isolation - each step does one thing; DEV_R038 shows HOW to share that one thing |
| **DM_R044** | Template structure; DEV_R038 extends with core function pattern |
| **DEV_R011** | Platform independence; DEV_R038 achieves this via parameterization |
| **SO_R007** | One function one file; core functions follow this rule |
| **MP064** | ETL-DRV separation; DEV_R038 applies within DRV layer |
| **MP145** | Doc-implementation alignment; wrapper headers document the pattern |

# Section 8: Migration Guide {#migration}

## Subsection 1: Identifying Candidates {#identifying-candidates}

Find derivation scripts with duplicated logic:

```bash
# Find platform-specific scripts that might share logic
ls scripts/update_scripts/DRV/cbz/cbz_D*.R
ls scripts/update_scripts/DRV/amz/amz_D*.R
ls scripts/update_scripts/DRV/eby/eby_D*.R

# Compare file sizes (similar sizes suggest shared logic)
wc -l scripts/update_scripts/DRV/*/[cae]by_D01_03.R
```

## Subsection 2: Extraction Steps {#extraction-steps}

1. **Analyze** all platform versions for common logic
2. **Design** platform_config structure to capture differences
3. **Extract** common logic to `DRV/all/fn_D{group}_{seq}_core.R`
4. **Create** thin wrappers for each platform
5. **Test** each platform wrapper
6. **Update** pipeline dependencies (targets, Makefile)

# Section 9: Example: D01 Implementation {#example}

## Subsection 1: Core Functions {#example-core}

**Location:** `scripts/global_scripts/16_derivations/`

| Core Function | Purpose | Key Entry Point |
|---------------|---------|-----------------|
| `fn_D01_03_core.R` | Customer DNA Analysis | `run_D01_03(platform_id, config)` |
| `fn_D01_04_core.R` | Customer Profile Creation | `run_D01_04(platform_id, config)` |
| `fn_D01_05_core.R` | App Views Generation | `run_D01_05(platform_id, config)` |

Key elements:
- `run_D01_{seq}(platform_id, config = NULL)` - main entry point with optional config
- Default table patterns when config not provided
- Calls algorithm functions from `global_scripts/04_utils/` (e.g., `fn_analysis_dna.R`)
- Platform-agnostic: works for any platform that provides `platform_id`

## Subsection 2: Platform Wrappers {#example-wrapper}

**Location:** `scripts/update_scripts/DRV/{platform}/`

| Wrapper | Core | Lines |
|---------|------|-------|
| `cbz_D01_03.R` | `fn_D01_03_core.R` | ~80 |
| `amz_D01_04.R` | `fn_D01_04_core.R` | ~80 |
| `eby_D01_05.R` | `fn_D01_05_core.R` | ~80 |

Key structure:
- Header with `CORE_FUNCTION: global_scripts/16_derivations/fn_D01_{seq}_core.R`
- `autoinit()` at start
- Source core from `file.path(GLOBAL_DIR, "16_derivations", "fn_D01_{seq}_core.R")`
- Call `run_D01_{seq}(platform_id = "{platform}")`
- Five-part structure: INITIALIZE → MAIN → TEST → SUMMARIZE → DEINITIALIZE
- `autodeinit()` at end

# Section 10: Conclusion {#conclusion}

The Derivation Core Function Pattern (DEV_R038) provides a structured approach to code reuse across platforms in derivation scripts. By extracting shared logic into core functions and using thin platform wrappers, this pattern:

1. **Eliminates duplication** - Single authoritative source for business logic
2. **Simplifies maintenance** - Bug fixes and enhancements in one place
3. **Enables testing** - Core functions can be tested independently
4. **Maintains flexibility** - Platform-specific configuration preserved
5. **Enforces standards** - Wrappers follow consistent template

Combined with DEV_R037 (Step Isolation), this pattern ensures derivation pipelines are both modular AND maintainable.
